summary: M2 dual-write parity gate delivered. dual_write_parity_gate.py runs the backfill twice (jsonl mode and dual mode) and asserts row count, sha256, canonicalWrites carry-over, artifactUri coverage, and run/source coverage parity. Errors are emitted to an error ledger; the gate fails when the feature flag flips read path to postgres. Read path defaults to jsonl. JSONL artifacts remain unchanged. Sample gate fixture and 3 smoke tests (16 assertions) included.

artifacts.description: run_parity_gate() drives backfill in jsonl + dual modes and compares row count, sha256, canonicalWrites, artifactUri, run/source coverage; structured errors with kind enum (row-count-mismatch, canonical-writes-drift, missing-artifact-uri, run-source-coverage-gap, sha256-drift, backfill-failure, read-path-flipped).
3 tests with 16+ assertions: gate ok=True on healthy manifest, gate ok=False when read-path flipped, per-table row count and sha256 parity, JSONL artifacts unchanged.

Sample gate output captured from the smoke run. Shows ok=true, rowCountChecks for 7 tables, sha256Checks for 7 tables, canonicalWritesCheck.ok=true, artifactUriCoverage with no missing URIs, runSourceCoverage matching expectedPairs.
Added WriteResult.backend_count so dual-mode merged results can be normalized to per-backend counts in parity reports.
Backfill parity report now records backendCount and computes parityOk against per-backend written + skipped; jsonl_counts pre-populated for pipeline_runs/source_runs.

acceptanceMapping.criterion: dual-write mode mirrors JSONL and PostgreSQL on small fixture / smoke run
acceptanceMapping.evidence: Smoke test builds 5-artifact lake (harvested, seed, card, anchor, proposal) and runs the gate; parityOk True for all 7 tables.

acceptanceMapping.criterion: parity gate checks row count, sha256, canonicalWrites, artifactUri, run/source coverage
acceptanceMapping.evidence: Gate returns rowCountChecks, sha256Checks, canonicalWritesCheck, artifactUriCoverage, runSourceCoverage in every report.

acceptanceMapping.criterion: parity failure does not affect JSONL output, errors written to ledger
acceptanceMapping.evidence: Smoke test verifies SHA256 of every JSONL fixture is unchanged after gate run; failure path returns errors[] structured ledger and non-zero exit.

acceptanceMapping.criterion: read path defaults to JSONL unless feature flag explicitly flipped
acceptanceMapping.evidence: feature_flag_read_path default 'jsonl'; gate emits ERROR_KIND_READ_PATH_FLIPPED when set to 'postgres'; second smoke test verifies.

nextActions: SANGUO-RAGOPS-0401: integrate this gate into large-run rehearsal mode matrix (dual-write mode)
SANGUO-RAGOPS-0402: add gate to governance regression harness as required smoke
SANGUO-RAGOPS-0501: gate ok=true is precondition for cutover decision packet
